focus: security boundaries across corpus retrieval, STUDY prompting, shared authority, and Open Design transport

findingDetails.title: Hydration has no contracted filesystem-containment boundary
findingDetails.evidence: The planned manifest and hydration CLI has generation guards but no canonical-root, traversal, or symlink acceptance criterion, so a conforming implementation may read outside styles/.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scoped exact search across phase 004/006/007/010 planning artifacts found no filesystem-containment control; phase 004 is the shared filesystem producer.

claimAdjudication.claim: The phase-004 contract permits hydration outside the styles root because no containment or symlink invariant is specified.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched all four assigned phase planning trees for realpath, canonicalization, traversal, escape, confinement, and symlink controls; reviewed generation and permitted-artifact language.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Manifest-derived fixed child paths or independent implementation containment may mitigate the risk, but neither is required or tested.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 after canonical root containment, explicit symlink policy, and negative traversal fixtures become binding acceptance criteria.

findingDetails.title: The STUDY injection envelope records a boundary but does not neutralize untrusted instructions
findingDetails.evidence: The planned transformer injects corpus-derived observations but specifies only an auditable envelope and literal/span leak gates, not instruction rejection or a data-only prompt boundary.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scoped search found envelope references across phase-006 artifacts but no behavioral prompt-injection criterion; phase-007 authority ordering does not sanitize prompt content.

claimAdjudication.claim: Corpus-borne instructions can influence generated prose while all planned phase-006 controls pass because the leak gate only tests copied values and spans.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched assigned phase trees for prompt injection, untrusted content, instruction rejection, sanitization, and boundary controls; reviewed de-literalization, locked FACTS, authority order, leak gates, and retry.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: A strict transformer could incidentally remove imperative text, but that behavior is neither specified nor tested.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 after a closed data-only observation schema, instruction rejection, and behavioral injection fixtures become binding.

findingRefinements.claim: Canonical packet surfaces give obsolete next actions and completion percentages for already-completed research phases.
findingRefinements.counterevidenceSought: Iteration 001 checked status tables, implementation summaries, parent phase map, planned child lifecycle fields, and graph metadata.
findingRefinements.alternativeExplanation: Resume precedence contains some runtime impact but does not remove contradictory canonical metadata.
findingRefinements.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if all validators and resume consumers exclude these spec continuity fields.

traceabilityChecks.notes: Generation and rights controls exist; filesystem containment is absent.
Anti-copy controls exist; behavioral prompt-injection resistance is absent.
Corpus and transport remain subordinate evidence.
Offline-first, no-cache, confirmation, and reconciliation controls are named.

noveltyJustification: weightedNew=10 for two new P1 findings; weightedRefinement=2.5 for the corrected carried-P1 packet; weightedTotal=15 across three active P1 findings; ratio=(10+2.5)/15=0.833333.
edgeCases: Structural-impact analysis unavailable under detached-lineage containment; graphless evidence used.
New findings concern missing planned acceptance criteria, not intentionally absent implementation.
Committed/local corpus reduces immediate exploitability, supporting P1 rather than P0.
The carried P1 was refined only to add findingId and was not duplicated as new.
ruledOut: Intentional non-implementation of phases 004/006/007/010 is not a finding.
Open Design transport is not an autonomous mutation authority under current or planned contracts.
Literal source copying is not wholly uncontrolled; the distinct gap is non-literal instruction influence.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Security-sensitive path, prompt-content, authority, and transport boundaries require producer/consumer and negative-control review.

targetSelection.selectionRationale: Phases 004/006/007/010, current Open Design transport, and prior carry-forward evidence were the assigned security surfaces.

searchLedger.result: generation/rights controls present; path containment absent
anti-copy controls present; instruction neutralization absent

searchLedger.surface: phase 007, phase 010, current Open Design skill
searchLedger.result: authority, no-cache, confirmation, and reconciliation controls confirmed
envelope references found; no path-containment or instruction-rejection contract found

searchLedger.surface: phase 004 hydration to phase 006 prompt and phase 007/010 handoff
searchLedger.result: two missing producer controls isolated without penalizing unimplemented consumers

detail: Corrected carried claim-adjudication packet now includes explicit findingId; severity unchanged.
Detached-lineage prompt required direct-read and exact-grep fallback.

reason: Phases 004/006/007/010 are explicitly planned; absence of runtime code was not penalized.
